title: Bommeria ehrenbergiana
intro: Bommeria ehrenbergiana, a species of fern, is a plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bommeria ehrenbergiana include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Bommeria ehrenbergiana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Bommeria ehrenbergiana is classified within the family Pteridaceae, commonly known as the maidenhair fern family. This family is part of the larger group of ferns, known as polypodiophytes. The genus Bommeria is further divided into a single species, B. ehrenbergiana, which is native to Mexico. The species is often categorized as a terrestrial fern, meaning it grows on or near the ground. It is also classified within the subfamily Cheiroglossodeae, which is a group of ferns characterized by their long, narrow fronds.
